title: Generators: ten frames, hundred charts, place-value charts
context: Ten frames (a 2x5 grid used for counting, addition, and subitising), hundred charts (a 10x10 grid of numbers 1-100 or 0-99), and place-value charts (columns for ones, tens, hundreds, thousands) are widely used across early and elementary mathematics. These three families share a common grid-based structure that the parametric engine can support with a generalised grid module. Relationship invariants are structural: grid cell counts and row/column alignment must be exact.
objective: Implement generator modules for ten frames, hundred charts, and place-value charts, building on the parametric engine, with verified structural correctness and math-educator review.
acceptanceCriteria: Ten-frame generator produces 2x5 and 5x2 orientations; blank and partially filled variants; configurable cell size.
Hundred-chart generator produces 1-100 and 0-99 starting variants; numbers correctly sequenced left-to-right, top-to-bottom; configurable cell size and font.
Place-value chart generator produces configurable column sets (ones/tens/hundreds; extending to millions); column labels are clear and legible in grayscale.
All three families pass the automated verification harness: cell counts, row/column alignment, and grid-line spacing within tolerance.
Math-educator review sign-off recorded for each family.
All standard variants generated (A4/Letter x colour/grayscale/ink-saver x standard/large-print).
MIT-licensed; all commits DCO-signed; passes pnpm build, pnpm test, and CI gate.
output: Three generator modules (ten-frames, hundred-charts, place-value-charts) integrated into the engine package, with spec files and example output PDFs, in a pull request, MIT-licensed.
